Quote: “Green Day — that’s when rock and roll started… the energy made me feel good.”
“Typically, I’m listening to metalcore and deathcore — that’s my flavor at the moment.”
“I listened to a ton of like metal, of course, hardcore, a lot of like deathcore stuff, metalcore stuff. I got into it originally—I was raised in the scene.”
“From that point on it went from pop punk to punk into hardcore… then prog metal…I love emo.”
Codes: GENRE_PREFERENCE(Pop Punk, Punk, Hardcore, Prog Metal,Emo)

Quote: “Post hardcore — I love the balance of super singy stuff with the heavy. Some bands pull it off great.”
“My sister showed me a lot of stuff that was on MTV when I was really young, like My Chemical Romance and then Slipknot on the more extreme side, and I just went down the rabbit hole after that.”
Codes: CHILDHOOD_INFLUENCE; GATEWAY_BAND; BAND_INFLUENCE(My Chemical Romance; Slipknot)

Quote: “I’m drawn more to metalcore and hardcore, but honestly I’m not picky — I just like metal music in general.”
“My dad played everything from Van Halen and Guns N’ Roses to Gwen Stefani and 80s pop like ‘Venus’ and ‘Two of Hearts,’ so I grew up around all kinds of genres.”
Codes: GENRE_PREFERENCE(80s pop, pop-punk, metalcore, emo, country); CHILDHOOD_INFLUENCE; CULTURAL_INFLUENCE

Quote: “I got into music just listening to whatever was on my mom’s iPod as a kid.”
“Honestly, I got into music through skateboarding back in 2014—just listening to emo music with my friends, and then it got heavier over time.”
“In middle school I got into emotional post-hardcore—Pierce the Veil, Black Veil Brides, Sleeping With Sirens.”
Codes: GENRE_PREFERENCE(post-hardcore); EMOTIONAL_CONNECTION; BAND_INFLUENCE(Pierce the Veil, Black Veil Brides, Sleeping With Sirens)

Quote: “My grandfather was a professional musician, and I grew up listening to him play upright bass and guitar — that was my first real exposure to music.”

"I had some friends that—they were in this local band while I was in middle school. I just loved watching them play and stuff. And then more and more of my friends started doing that, getting into music, and then finally got into heavier stuff as well."
“When I was 14, I started playing the guitar and that’s when I really started to learn how to play music that I wanted to play.”
“Linkin Park… Chester Bennington — he inspired me to want to be a singer.”
“I was a chorus nerd growing up… sang in all of the school choirs, got into show choir.”

So it started mainly because of my sister and my brother-in-law. They introduced me to some of the things that I listen to now. So it started mostly with like punk genre and kind of evolved into, hey, check out this band. And then I started listening to stuff like Korn, which is more like metal related.
I think my dad started playing—was taking guitar lessons when I was like eight or something. I guess I just thought it looked cool, and I started playing guitar.
